D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Madder Root — Alizarin and Turkey Red

Rubia tinctorum roots yield alizarin (1,2-dihydroxyanthraquinone); the British army's red coats used Turkey-red madder dye until synthetic alizarin replaced it in 1869

Alizarin is a mordant dye — it binds to fibre only through a metal salt (alum gives red, iron gives purple-black). Madder paid the British army's coat budget for two centuries. In 1869 Graebe and Liebermann synthesised it from anthracene, collapsing the natural madder industry in three years.

Toggle mordant: Al³⁺ gives Turkey red, Fe²⁺ gives aubergine purple, Cr³⁺ gives garnet.
